title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek public's help identifying a second juvenile in a South Boston lemonade stand robbery.
Surveillance photos show the suspect wearing black pants, black hat, no shirt, and carrying a black backpack.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested; police continue to investigate the robbery that occurred on June 10.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering leaving the Lakers and is evaluating potential NBA teams for his next, likely final, season.
Boston Celtics, featuring Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens, are listed as a possible, but not favored, destination for James.
James's decision could place him in Boston's starting lineup with Derrick White, Paul George, Mitchell Robinson, and Tatum.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes the Boston Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish likens the trade to the controversial Luka Doncic move by the Dallas Mavericks in February 2025.
Parish expresses hope that Jaylen Brown and the Celtics will maintain an amicable relationship going forward.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown after nine seasons together with the Boston Celtics.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and multiple draft picks.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics, despite not requesting the trade.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Neemias Queta signs a four-year, $56 million extension with the Boston Celtics, extending his contract through the 2030-31 season.
The Celtics picked up Queta's $2,667,944 option for the 2026-27 season before adding the four-year extension.
Queta was a standout player last season, finishing fourth in the Most Improved Player Award voting with 10.2 points and 8.4 rebounds per game.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The restaurant's building, designated a National Historic Landmark in 2003, dates back to the early 1700s.
Master chef Americo DiFronzo serves classic dishes, selling up to 1,500 meals daily, including 10,000 gallons of clam chowder annually.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4 will feature performances and a drone show, with Storrow Drive closed to vehicles.
On July 9, a World Cup quarterfinal at Boston Stadium will cause anticipated rush hour traffic on Route 1 and around Foxborough.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail commences on July 11, with over 60 tall ships, including the USS Constitution, entering Boston Harbor.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, Boston's historic restaurant, has hosted six U.S. presidents including JFK, Obama, and Clinton.
A 2003 painting by Stan Kotzen depicts over 50 famous visitors like Al Pacino, Clint Eastwood, and Matt Damon.
Current owner, Joseph Milano, highlights the storied tradition of celebrities dining at the iconic Boston establishment.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year New Hampshire State Police veteran, passed away unexpectedly at age 41.
Ross served as commander of the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit; he leaves behind a wife and three children.
A fund for Ross's family has been established, and public visiting hours are set for July 6 in Hampton.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed disappointment over his trade from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown criticized Celtics President Brad Stevens for poor communication during trade negotiations.
The Celtics traded Brown for Paul George, draft picks, hoping to improve their roster.
